# DeutschFlow — Lesson Template

**Version:** 1.0 · **Updated:** 2026-07-12 · **Status:** Active — use for every DeutschFlow lesson.
**Based on:** `docs/lessons/A1/lesson-01-greetings-and-introductions.md` · LESSON_BLUEPRINT

> Copy this template, fill every `<!-- -->` placeholder, and pass the `lesson-checklist.md` review. Do not skip sections — missing sections cause inconsistent learner experiences.

---

```markdown
# {LEVEL} · Unit {U} · Lesson {N} — {TITLE}
### {German subtitle — the capability phrased in German}

**Level:** {A1/A2/B1/B2/C1} · **Unit:** {U} "{UNIT_THEME}" · **Lesson:** {N} of {UNIT_LESSON_COUNT}
**Capability:** *{ONE English sentence describing what the learner can DO after this lesson — see §1}*
**Duration:** ~{X} min · **New items:** {N} · **Grammar pattern:** {N} ({NAME})
**Status:** [ ] Draft  [ ] Reviewed  [ ] Has audio  [ ] Native-checked
```

---

## 1. Learning objective

<!-- One sentence. Must pass the LESSON_BLUEPRINT test: "After this lesson you can ____" with a real-world action. -->
By the end, a complete beginner can, unaided:
- **{do this}**
- **{do that}**
<!-- 3–5 bullet verbs, not topics. "Order a coffee", not "Learn accusative". -->

## 2. Success criteria
<!-- Must satisfy all five. Answer YES or explain why not. -->
- [ ] **Completable** by a zero-German learner without external help
- [ ] Learner can **produce** {key sentence} from memory
- [ ] Learner **recognizes** all vocabulary in the Mini Review
- [ ] Learner **feels** "{I can now ____}" (capability feeling, not completion feeling)
- [ ] Items are **seeded to SRS**

---

## 3. Dialogue

<!-- Author the dialogue FIRST, then extract vocabulary and grammar from it. -->
**Scene:** {brief setting — café, street, home, etc.}
**Speakers:** {2–3}

| # | Speaker | German | 🔊 Pronunciation (for A1–A2) | English *(tap to reveal)* |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | {Name} | **{line}** | {respelling} | {English} |
| … | | | | |
| 6–10 | | | | |

---

## 4. Vocabulary

<!-- 8–12 words, chunked into sets of ≤6. Every noun → article + plural. Every item → a "watch out" note. -->

### Set 1 (of {N})
| German | Art/Pl | 🔊 | Meaning | Example | Common beginner confusion |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| | | | | | |

<!-- Repeat per set. These become the front/back of flip cards. -->

---

## 5. Pattern Discovery (Grammar)

### Notice
<!-- 2–3 lines pulled from the Dialogue showing the pattern in context. -->
*"{dialogue line}"* · *"{dialogue line}"*

### Name it
<!-- Plain-English name + compact table + one why-line. -->
| {column} | {column} |
|---|---|
| | |

**Why:** {one sentence}

### Emma "Explain more" (on demand)
<!-- Optional. Emma expands only if the learner asks. -->

---

## 6. Guided Practice

### Exercise 1 — {type: multiple-choice / matching / ordering / fill-in / listening / micro-production}
- **Type:**
- **Purpose:**
- **Prompt:**
- **Answer:**
- **Distractors (if MCQ):**
- **Expected common mistake:**
- **Feedback ✓:** 
- **Feedback ✗:** 
- **Emma hint:**
<!-- Repeat for 3–6 exercises. Core set = 4; expand per LESSON_BLUEPRINT count. -->

---

## 7. Speaking (Emma) — optional

- **Emma prompt:** {what Emma says to open}
- **Suggested response chip:** {scaffold}
- **Branch A (correct):** {Emma's reply → next turn or close}
- **Branch B (English / incomplete):** {Emma's redirection}
- **Branch C (stuck / silent):** {Emma's encouragement + chip}
- **Skip path:** {Emma's graceful exit}
<!-- For 2+ turns, repeat the branches. -->

---

## 8. Mini Review

<!-- 3–4 rapid items, mix of recognition + recall, ending on wins. -->
| # | Type | Prompt | Answer |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | {recognition / cued-recall / listening / light-prod} | {prompt} | {correct} |
| … | | | |

---

## 9. Celebration

> 🎉 {One sentence naming the capability achieved.}
> "{the real-world outcome}"
> 🔥 {positive-only streak reinforcement}
> **[ See what you learned → ]**

**Emma:** {1 sentence — celebrate the capability, not the completion.}

---

## 10. Learning Summary

- 📇 **{N} words & phrases** → added to your review deck
- ✦ **1 pattern:** {name}
- 🗣 **{N} conversation{s}** practiced with Emma
- **{N} cards are ready to review**
> **Up next:** Lesson {N+1} — {capability of the next lesson}
> **[ Next lesson → ]   [ Review now ]**

---

## 11. Emma intervention inventory
<!-- One-line purpose per Emma touchpoint in this lesson. Authoring check: is the hint ladder followed? Does she ever answer first? -->
| Stage | Purpose | Hint ladder rung |
|---|---|---|
| Welcome | First contact | — |
| Pattern Discovery | Optionally explain the rule | Rung 2 (explain) |
| Exercise N | {prompt} | Rung 1 (encourage) |
| Speaking | Open + coach | Rungs 0–3 |
| Celebration | Celebrate the capability | — |

---

## 12. Common beginner mistakes
| Mistake | Why it happens | Fix in-lesson |
|---|---|---|
| {typical error} | {reason} | {which exercise/stage recovers it} |

---

## 13. Timing
| Stage | Time |
|---|---|
| 1 Warm Welcome | |
| 2 Dialogue | |
| 3 Vocabulary | |
| 4 Pattern Discovery | |
| 5 Guided Practice | |
| 6 Speaking (optional) | |
| 7 Mini Review | |
| 8–9 Celebration + Summary | |
| **Total** | **~{X} min** |

---

## 14. Assumptions & edge cases
- **Audio:** …
- **Vocabulary:** …
- **Edge case:** {learners who already know parts of this, offline, etc.}
```

## Using this template

1. **Fill it top to bottom** — never skip a section.
2. **Author the Dialogue first** — then extract Vocab and the Pattern from it.
3. **Run `lesson-checklist.md`** on the completed lesson.
4. **Score yourself** with `lesson-review-rubric.md` (minimum target: 4.0 average, no category below 3).
5. **Native review** before marking "reviewed".
6. **Author the Emma script items** using `emma-script-template.md` for voice consistency.
7. **Refer to `exercise-pattern-library.md`** for the canonical exercise types; do not invent new ones without adding them to the library.

### Template revision log
- 1.0 (2026-07-12): Initial, based on reference Lesson 1.
